TEXT.citation: Wikipedia contributors. (2026). Sakizaya Wikipedia. Wikimedia Foundation. Retrieved 2026-06-09, from https://szy.wikipedia.org/
TEXT.BibTeX_citation: @misc{Wiki_szy, title = {Sakizaya Wikipedia}, author = {{Wikipedia contributors}}, year = {2026}, publisher = {Wikimedia Foundation}, url = {https://szy.wikipedia.org/}, urldate = {2026-06-09} }

S.FORM: == landaway bunac == u sulit nu Hulam mauzip mabulak i 500-1200m a buyu , uyaan a kilankilangan. i dihep i lunabay, hali malalemeday lutulutukan a lala'. akenek ku sanek, siledek. kapah tu misapayu' tu makalat tu bau atu cilekay. u takalaw sa 40-90cm, u yadah a mihcaay a lamit. u heci a kulit sa u sumanahay. mulu ku heci. siheci i 8-10 bulad. u siwkay nu nipaluma u sanek nu nipaluma malaheci tu imelang pasubana i cacudadan a lacul u zuma a cidekay a kamu, saungay misapayu' a cidekay u sulit nu zuma a kamu-Hulam atu Amilika u sulit nu Amilika Paris polyphylla is an Asian species of plants native to China, the Indian Subcontinent, and Indochina. It produces spider-like flowers that throw out long, thread-like, yellowish green petals throughout most of the warm summer months and into the autumn. In the fall, the flowers are followed by small, scarlet berries. It is a perennial, which slowly spreads, is fully hardy in Britain, and survives in leafy, moist soil in either complete or partial shade. This flowering plant usually grows up to 90 cm (3 ft) high and spreads out about 30 cm (1 ft) wide. Its leaves grow in a single whorl below a flower growing in two whorls. It is used as an ornamental plant for woodland gardens or for planting under deciduous trees.
S.PHON: == r~…æandawaj buna ¶ == u sur~…æit nu ƒßur~…æam mauzip mabur~…æak i ***-****m a buju , ujaan a kir~…æankir~…æa≈ãan. i diƒß…ôp i r~…æunabaj, ƒßar~…æi mar~…æar~…æ…ôm…ôdaj r~…æutur~…æutukan a r~…æar~…æa î~ °. ak…ôn…ôk ku san…ôk, sir~…æ…ôd…ôk. kapaƒß tu misapaju î~ ° tu makar~…æat tu bau atu ¶ir~…æ…ôkaj. u takar~…æaw sa **-** ¶m, u jadaƒß a miƒß ¶aaj a r~…æamit. u ƒß…ô ¶i a kur~…æit sa u sumanaƒßaj. mur~…æu ku ƒß…ô ¶i. siƒß…ô ¶i i *-** bur~…æad. u siwkaj nu nipar~…æuma u san…ôk nu nipar~…æuma mar~…æaƒß…ô ¶i tu im…ôr~…æa≈ã pasubana i ¶a ¶udadan a r~…æa ¶ur~…æ u zuma a ¶id…ôkaj a kamu, sau≈ãaj misapaju î~ ° a ¶id…ôkaj u sur~…æit nu zuma a kamu-ƒßur~…æam atu amir~…æika u sur~…æit nu amir~…æika paris por~…æjpƒßjr~…ær~…æa is an asian sp…ô ¶i…ôs of pr~…æants nati*…ô to ¶ƒßina, tƒß…ô indian sub ¶ontin…ônt, and indo ¶ƒßina. it produ ¶…ôs spid…ôr-r~…æik…ô fr~…æow…ôrs tƒßat tƒßrow out r~…æo≈ã, tƒßr…ôad-r~…æik…ô, j…ôr~…ær~…æowisƒß gr…ô…ôn p…ôtar~…æs tƒßrougƒßout most of tƒß…ô warm summ…ôr montƒßs and into tƒß…ô autumn. in tƒß…ô far~…ær~…æ, tƒß…ô fr~…æow…ôrs ar…ô for~…ær~…æow…ôd bj smar~…ær~…æ, s ¶arr~…æ…ôt b…ôrri…ôs. it is a p…ôr…ônniar~…æ, wƒßi ¶ƒß sr~…æowr~…æj spr…ôads, is fur~…ær~…æj ƒßardj in britain, and sur*i*…ôs in r~…æ…ôafj, moist soir~…æ in …ôitƒß…ôr ¶ompr~…æ…ôt…ô or partiar~…æ sƒßad…ô. tƒßis fr~…æow…ôri≈ã pr~…æant usuar~…ær~…æj grows up to ** ¶m (* ft) ƒßigƒß and spr…ôads out about ** ¶m (* ft) wid…ô. its r~…æ…ôa*…ôs grow in a si≈ãr~…æ…ô wƒßorr~…æ b…ôr~…æow a fr~…æow…ôr growi≈ã in two wƒßorr~…æs. it is us…ôd as an ornam…ôntar~…æ pr~…æant for woodr~…æand gard…ôns or for pr~…æanti≈ã und…ôr d…ô ¶iduous tr…ô…ôs.
S.FORM: == landaway bunac == u sulit nu Hulam mauzip mabulak i 500-1200m a buyu , uyaan a kilankilangan. i dihep i lunabay, hali malalemeday lutulutukan a lala'. akenek ku sanek, siledek. kapah tu misapayu' tu makalat tu bau atu cilekay. u takalaw sa 40-90cm, u yadah a mihcaay a lamit. u heci a kulit sa u sumanahay. mulu ku heci. siheci i 8-10 bulad. u siwkay nu nipaluma u sanek nu nipaluma malaheci tu imelang pasubana i cacudadan a lacul u zuma a cidekay a kamu, saungay misapayu' a cidekay u sulit nu zuma a kamu-Hulam atu Amilika u sulit nu Amilika Paris polyphylla is an Asian species of plants native to China, the Indian Subcontinent, and Indochina. It produces spider-like flowers that throw out long, thread-like, yellowish green petals throughout most of the warm summer months and into the autumn. In the fall, the flowers are followed by small, scarlet berries. It is a perennial, which slowly spreads, is fully hardy in Britain, and survives in leafy, moist soil in either complete or partial shade. This flowering plant usually grows up to 90 cm (3 ft) high and spreads out about 30 cm (1 ft) wide. Its leaves grow in a single whorl below a flower growing in two whorls. It is used as an ornamental plant for woodland gardens or for planting under deciduous trees.
S.PHON: == r~…æandawaj buna ¶ == u sur~…æit nu ƒßur~…æam mauzip mabur~…æak i ***-****m a buju , ujaan a kir~…æankir~…æa≈ãan. i diƒß…ôp i r~…æunabaj, ƒßar~…æi mar~…æar~…æ…ôm…ôdaj r~…æutur~…æutukan a r~…æar~…æa î~ °. ak…ôn…ôk ku san…ôk, sir~…æ…ôd…ôk. kapaƒß tu misapaju î~ ° tu makar~…æat tu bau atu ¶ir~…æ…ôkaj. u takar~…æaw sa **-** ¶m, u jadaƒß a miƒß ¶aaj a r~…æamit. u ƒß…ô ¶i a kur~…æit sa u sumanaƒßaj. mur~…æu ku ƒß…ô ¶i. siƒß…ô ¶i i *-** bur~…æad. u siwkaj nu nipar~…æuma u san…ôk nu nipar~…æuma mar~…æaƒß…ô ¶i tu im…ôr~…æa≈ã pasubana i ¶a ¶udadan a r~…æa ¶ur~…æ u zuma a ¶id…ôkaj a kamu, sau≈ãaj misapaju î~ ° a ¶id…ôkaj u sur~…æit nu zuma a kamu-ƒßur~…æam atu amir~…æika u sur~…æit nu amir~…æika paris por~…æjpƒßjr~…ær~…æa is an asian sp…ô ¶i…ôs of pr~…æants nati*…ô to ¶ƒßina, tƒß…ô indian sub ¶ontin…ônt, and indo ¶ƒßina. it produ ¶…ôs spid…ôr-r~…æik…ô fr~…æow…ôrs tƒßat tƒßrow out r~…æo≈ã, tƒßr…ôad-r~…æik…ô, j…ôr~…ær~…æowisƒß gr…ô…ôn p…ôtar~…æs tƒßrougƒßout most of tƒß…ô warm summ…ôr montƒßs and into tƒß…ô autumn. in tƒß…ô far~…ær~…æ, tƒß…ô fr~…æow…ôrs ar…ô for~…ær~…æow…ôd bj smar~…ær~…æ, s ¶arr~…æ…ôt b…ôrri…ôs. it is a p…ôr…ônniar~…æ, wƒßi ¶ƒß sr~…æowr~…æj spr…ôads, is fur~…ær~…æj ƒßardj in britain, and sur*i*…ôs in r~…æ…ôafj, moist soir~…æ in …ôitƒß…ôr ¶ompr~…æ…ôt…ô or partiar~…æ sƒßad…ô. tƒßis fr~…æow…ôri≈ã pr~…æant usuar~…ær~…æj grows up to ** ¶m (* ft) ƒßigƒß and spr…ôads out about ** ¶m (* ft) wid…ô. its r~…æ…ôa*…ôs grow in a si≈ãr~…æ…ô wƒßorr~…æ b…ôr~…æow a fr~…æow…ôr growi≈ã in two wƒßorr~…æs. it is us…ôd as an ornam…ôntar~…æ pr~…æant for woodr~…æand gard…ôns or for pr~…æanti≈ã und…ôr d…ô ¶iduous tr…ô…ôs.
